behavior: Host startup can fetch the official OpenAI model catalog once and keep it as a reusable runtime guard input.
runCodexCliExecPlan checks the selected plan.model against a supplied modelCatalog before spawning Codex CLI.
If requireModelCatalog is true and no catalog is supplied, execution is blocked with codex_cli_model_catalog_required before spawn.
If the selected model is absent from the supplied catalog, execution is blocked with codex_cli_model_unavailable:<model> before spawn.
Successful runs include modelAvailability evidence in the run result.

coveredScenarios.name: startup_catalog_check
coveredScenarios.result: fetch wrapper is called once and returns a reusable startup catalog check

coveredScenarios.name: model_availability
coveredScenarios.result: available, missing, and required-catalog states are reported

coveredScenarios.name: pre_spawn_runner_gate
coveredScenarios.result: runner spawns only when the selected model is present in the supplied catalog

limitations: No live OpenAI /models request was executed for this evidence file.
No API key or Authorization header value is recorded.
The feature is exposed as library-level startup and pre-run gates; a separate CLI command can be added for operator-triggered checks.
